food_subgroup: Deep-fried half-moon pastry with spiced sardine filling

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for Epok-Epok. Closest parent is fried pastry with fish filling. No exact URI available; null is appropriate.
ontology.foodex2_description: Deep-fried half-moon pastry with spiced sardine filling (Malay epok-epok style)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0FRB is the closest defensible FoodEx2 parent for fried filled pastry. No exact epok-epok code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for this specific Malay snack food.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard epok-epok piece weighs approximately 80g, as commonly sold at Malay hawker stalls and Ramadan bazaars in Singapore and Malaysia.

nutrient_source.title: Epok-Epok (sardine-filled Malay curry puff) — HPB Singapore Foodo
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for sardine-filled epok-epok. Values represent the composite fried pastry with sardine filling as consumed.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of wheat flour-based fried pastry — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for epok-epok. GI of 65 is estimated from wheat flour shortcrust/flaky pastry analogues in the University of Sydney GI Database (GI range 60–72 for refined wheat flour pastry products). The sardine filling protein and fat content moderates glycaemic response relative to plain flour snacks. GL per 80g serving calculated as (65 × 23.6g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 15.3, rounded to 15.4 consistent with source record.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium from canned sardines in tomato sauce and pastry preparation
hypertension.guidance: A single 80g epok-epok delivers approximately 384mg sodium (16% of the 2,400mg daily limit), and consuming two pieces — a common snack portion — provides 768mg sodium (32% of daily limit), making portion control essential for individuals managing hypertension.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium GI (65) refined wheat flour pastry with moderate glycaemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: One 80g piece delivers approximately 23.6g carbohydrate with a glycaemic load of ~15.4; the sardine filling provides protein and omega-3 fat that moderately attenuates the glycaemic response, but individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit to one piece per occasion and pair with non-starchy vegetables or a protein source.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from deep-frying and trans fat from frying oil, partially offset by omega-3 fatty acids from sardines
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each 80g piece contributes approximately 4.6g saturated fat and 0.16g trans fat from deep-frying, alongside beneficial EPA/DHA omega-3 fatty acids and vitamin D from sardines; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency of consumption and prefer baked or air-fried alternatives when available.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Omega-3 fatty acids and vitamin D from sardines are anti-inflammatory, but deep-frying generates pro-inflammatory advanced glycation end-products (AGEs)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Sardines provide EPA/DHA omega-3 fatty acids and vitamin D (1.5mcg/100g) beneficial for joint health, but the deep-frying process generates pro-inflammatory AGEs; moderate consumption is acceptable within an overall anti-inflammatory dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Epok-Epok Sardin is a golden, crispy deep-fried half-moon pastry that is a staple of Malay hawker culture in Singapore and Malaysia. The pastry shell is made from a simple shortcrust wheat flour dough, crimped into a distinctive crescent shape and deep-fried until golden brown and crunchy. The filling consists of canned sardines in tomato sauce sautéed with sliced onions, bird's eye chillies, and sometimes a squeeze of lime juice, producing a spicy, tangy, and umami-rich interior that contrasts with the crispy exterior. Unlike the Chinese curry puff (which uses a potato and hard-boiled egg filling), epok-epok is distinguished by its sardine filling and slightly more intense, spicy flavour profile. Nutritionally, each 80g piece provides approximately 238 kcal, 7.6g protein, 13.2g fat, and 23.6g carbohydrate. The sardine filling makes epok-epok a meaningful source of omega-3 fatty acids (EPA/DHA), vitamin B12 (2.0mcg/100g), vitamin D (1.5mcg/100g), calcium (92mg/100g), and selenium (14mcg/100g). Sodium is moderately high at 480mg/100g due to canned sardines and pastry seasoning. The glycaemic index is estimated at 65 (medium), with a glycaemic load of approximately 15.4 per 80g serving. Epok-Epok is commonly sold at Malay hawker stalls, Geylang Serai market, Ramadan bazaars, and school canteens, typically as a breakfast snack or tea-time treat alongside lontong and bubur lambuk. It is halal and dairy-free.
common_user_queries: epok epok vs curry puff difference

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo, the primary approved source for Singapore dishes. The food is an exact match in the HPB database. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database wheat flour pastry data, clearly documented. All mandatory multilingual names for a Singaporean dish are present.
notes: GI value of 65 is an analogous estimate based on wheat flour shortcrust/flaky pastry data from the University of Sydney GI Database (GI range 60–72); no direct GI measurement for epok-epok exists in the literature. Monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat values are not reported by the HPB source and are set to -1. Water content is not reported by the HPB source and is set to -1. Two portions were present in the input (80g and 40g); the 80g piece is used as the default serving size consistent with HPB and common hawker portion. Trans fat of 0.2g/100g reflects residual trans fat from deep-frying oil; this is a low but non-zero value consistent with deep-fried pastry products.
